page: The Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized server instance belonging to the Laosv4 family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The configuration features 24 virtual CPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 17,279 GB of local storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark testing, the L24aos_v4 demonstrates strong single-core and multi-core performance. Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, along with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, rank in the top 25%. For specialized tasks, the server achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference text generation using a small 135M model, alongside strong performance in image and text processing. Conversely, multi-core stress-ng performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations on Passmark fall into the average tier. The server exhibits a performance tradeoff when handling larger workloads, as demonstrated by its weak performance in LLM inference prompt processing for a 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L24aos_v4 offers high resource density, combining substantial memory capacity with an exceptionally large local storage footprint. This makes it highly suitable for data-intensive workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. The hardware profile is optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, heavy text and image processing, and local file caching. It is also well-suited for running lightweight machine learning inference tasks, though it is less optimal for massive-scale LLM deployments.
description: The Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Operating at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it features 24 vCPUs, 12 physical cores, and 192.0 GB of RAM. A defining characteristic is its massive 17,279 GB of local storage, backed by Premium Storage capability under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark results show strong single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. However, memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, and performance drops significantly for large-scale LLM inference. This instance is highly suited for large databases, data warehousing, and text or image processing applications requiring substantial local storage and memory capacity without GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 17,279 GB of local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor for demanding database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage-optimized server with 24 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 17.2 TB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and massive local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores
192.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Massive 17,279 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference
Optimized for large databases, data warehousing, and heavy text or image processing
